exercises.title: Parameter Set Comparison
exercises.description: Create a comprehensive comparison of FN-DSA parameter sets.
tasks: Create a table comparing FN-DSA-512 and FN-DSA-1024
Calculate the ratio of sizes and performance between variants
Compare with ML-DSA-44 and ML-DSA-87 equivalents
Identify the security level gap (no Category 3)
Recommend how to handle Category 3 requirements
hints: Consider both size and performance trade-offs

exercises.title: Bandwidth Impact Calculator
exercises.description: Build a tool to calculate FN-DSA bandwidth requirements.
tasks: Calculate signature overhead for N messages per day
Compare FN-DSA-512 vs ML-DSA-44 for TLS handshakes
Calculate certificate chain overhead (3 signatures)
Estimate annual bandwidth savings with FN-DSA
Generate recommendations based on bandwidth constraints
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class BandwidthProfile:
    name: str
    pk_bytes: int
    sig_bytes: int

SCHEMES = {
    'ECDSA-P256': BandwidthProfile('ECDSA-P256', 64, 64),
    'FN-DSA-512': BandwidthProfile('FN-DSA-512', 897, 666),
    'FN-DSA-1024': BandwidthProfile('FN-DSA-1024', 1793, 1280),
    'ML-DSA-44': BandwidthProfile('ML-DSA-44', 1312, 2420),
    'ML-DSA-65': BandwidthProfile('ML-DSA-65', 1952, 3293),
}

def calculate_daily_bandwidth(scheme: str, messages_per_day: int,
                               include_pk: bool = False) -> dict:
    """
    Calculate daily bandwidth for signature scheme.
    """
    profile = SCHEMES[scheme]
    sig_bytes = profile.sig_bytes * messages_per_day
    pk_bytes = profile.pk_bytes if include_pk else 0
    total = sig_bytes + pk_bytes
    
    return {
        'scheme': scheme,
        'daily_sig_bytes': sig_bytes,
        'daily_sig_mb': sig_bytes / (1024 * 1024),
        'pk_bytes': pk_bytes,
        'total_bytes': total,
    }

def certificate_chain_overhead(scheme: str, chain_length: int = 3) -> int:
    """
    Calculate certificate chain signature overhead.
    Each cert has issuer's public key + issuer's signature.
    """
    profile = SCHEMES[scheme]
    # Each cert in chain (except leaf) contributes PK + Sig
    overhead = chain_length * profile.sig_bytes + (chain_length - 1) * profile.pk_bytes
    return overhead

# Analysis
print("FN-DSA Bandwidth Analysis")
print("=" * 50)

messages_per_day = 1_000_000

for scheme in ['FN-DSA-512', 'ML-DSA-44']:
    result = calculate_daily_bandwidth(scheme, messages_per_day)
    print(f"\n{scheme} ({messages_per_day:,} messages/day):")
    print(f"  Daily signature data: {result['daily_sig_mb']:.1f} MB")

print("\nCertificate chain overhead (3-cert chain):")
for scheme in SCHEMES:
    overhead = certificate_chain_overhead(scheme)
    print(f"  {scheme}: {overhead:,} bytes")
hints: TLS handshake sends leaf + intermediate certs
Consider both signature and public key sizes
1 million handshakes/day is common for busy servers

exercises.title: Use Case Decision Matrix
exercises.description: Create a decision matrix for FN-DSA vs alternatives.
tasks: List 10 different use case scenarios
Score FN-DSA-512/1024 vs ML-DSA for each
Consider: size, speed, implementation complexity, security
Identify where FN-DSA clearly wins or loses
Create a flowchart for algorithm selection
hints: FN-DSA wins on size, loses on key generation speed

exercises.title: Security Level Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze FN-DSA security levels and the Category 3 gap.
tasks: Research the Core-SVP methodology for security estimation
Calculate effective security bits for both FN-DSA variants
Analyze why n=768 (Category 3) doesn't exist
Compare BKZ block sizes required for each variant
Propose strategy for applications needing exactly Category 3
hints: Core-SVP considers best known quantum attacks
BKZ block size β determines attack cost
